page: Amazon Web Services c5.xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon 8124M cores running at 3.4 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 24 MB L3 cache, with support for AVX and AVX-512F vector instructions. The system is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate moderate single-core performance alongside weaker multi-core and memory throughput. The single-core stress-ng and Geekbench tests score in the average tier, supported by average single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression rates. However, multi-core performance is constrained, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores falling into the weak category. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with large-block read and write speeds categorized as weak, and the Passmark memory score rated as poor. Database operations, Redis SET throughput, and static web serving benchmarks similarly reflect weak performance tiers.

The c5.xlarge offers a dedicated compute profile with a low memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads that require consistent CPU allocation without high memory demands. The lack of bundled local storage means users must rely on external network storage, which, combined with the 1 Gbps baseline network speed, may limit I/O-heavy applications. This server is best suited for compute-bound tasks that do not require massive parallelization or large memory footprints, such as lightweight web serving, basic text processing, and single-threaded batch processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c5.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 2 physical Intel Xeon 8124M cores running at 3.4 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs, and operates with a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows average single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance, though multi-core execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into weaker performance tiers. This configuration represents a cost-efficient option for dedicated compute tasks with low memory requirements. It is best suited for workloads such as lightweight web serving, text processing, and single-threaded batch processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5.xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a 1 Gbps baseline network, designed for light compute-bound workloads.

tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and a balanced memory profile for single-threaded workloads.

8.0 GB of DDR4 memory with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Average single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
No bundled local storage and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Best suited for lightweight web serving and single-threaded batch processing
